title: Prefer task-based concurrency over manual thread creation
messageFormat: Schedule this work with Task or the thread pool instead of creating a Thread directly.
summary: Constructing a Thread by hand allocates a dedicated OS thread, bypasses the managed thread pool, and leaves you to coordinate lifetime, exceptions, and results yourself. Most workloads are better expressed with Task.Run or the higher-level task abstractions, which pool threads, propagate exceptions, and compose with async/await.
noncompliant:
var worker = new Thread(DoWork); // Noncompliant
worker.Start();
